channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Ultraclean monolayers from a van der Waals confinement
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 09 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02503-y

Growing transition metal dichalcogenide monolayers at a buried van der Waals interface simultaneously improves thickness control, preserves ultraclean interfaces, and enables intrinsic patterning and atomically selective Janus structure formation.
item.dc:title: Ultraclean monolayers from a van der Waals confinement
item.dc:creator: Chang-Hsun Huang
Jui-Han Fu
Vincent Tung

item.title: Atomically precise synthesis and simultaneous heterostructure integration of 2D transition metal dichalcogenides through nano-confinement
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 09 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02495-9

Nano-confinement between a capping layer composed of graphene or hexagonal boron nitride and a substrate directs the growth kinetics during chemical vapour deposition, which enables the atomically precise synthesis of 2D transition metal dichalcogenide monolayers, Janus monolayers and van der Waals heterostructures with ultraclean interfaces.
item.dc:title: Atomically precise synthesis and simultaneous heterostructure integration of 2D transition metal dichalcogenides through nano-confinement
item.dc:creator: Ce Bian
Yifan Zhao
Roger Guzman
Hongtao Liu
Hao Hu
Qi Qi
Ke Zhu
Hao Wang
Kang Wu
Hui Guo
Wanzhen He
Zhaoqing Wang
Peng Peng
Zhiping Xu
Wu Zhou
Feng Ding
Haitao Yang
Hong-Jun Gao

item.title: Narrowband quantum emitters with optically addressable spin states generated in hexagonal boron nitride
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02474-6

A single-step thermal process reliably generates high-density, narrowband quantum emitters in hexagonal boron nitride. More than 25% of these emitters show room-temperature optical spin readout, revealing S = 1 and S = 1/2 spin complexes that are explained by charge transfer from strongly to weakly coupled spin pairs.
item.dc:title: Narrowband quantum emitters with optically addressable spin states generated in hexagonal boron nitride

item.title: Author Correction: How charge frustration causes ion ordering and microphase separation at surfaces
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 05 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02525-6

Author Correction: How charge frustration causes ion ordering and microphase separation at surfaces
item.dc:title: Author Correction: How charge frustration causes ion ordering and microphase separation at surfaces
item.dc:creator: Mingyi Zhang
Benjamin A. Legg
Benjamin A. Helfrecht
Yuanzhong Zhang
Shuai Tan
Ying Xia
Rae Karell Yodong
Monica Iepure
Venkateshkumar Prabhakaran
Peter J. Pauzauskie
Younjin Min
Christopher J. Mundy
James J. De Yoreo

item.title: A 3-GPa ductile martensitic alloy enabled by interface complexes and dislocations
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 30 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02479-9

A martensitic alloy with a tensile strength exceeding 3 GPa and a fracture elongation of 5.13% is developed. These mechanical properties arise from interface complexes interacting with dense dislocation networks, which is a mechanism shown to be applicable to other compositions.
item.dc:title: A 3-GPa ductile martensitic alloy enabled by interface complexes and dislocations
item.dc:creator: Rong Lv
Jia Li
Yunzhu Shi
Shuai Dai
Shuo Wang
Xinren Chen
Xiaoye Zhou
Fei Zhang
Meiyuan Jiao
Chao Ma
Alexander Schökel
Shaolou Wei
Yan Ma
Claudio Pistidda
Zhifeng Lei
Zhaoping Lu

item.title: A free energy landscape analysis of resistance fluctuations in a memristive device
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 30 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02487-9

Resistance noise in memristive devices is often described as a thermally activated process across simple energy barriers, but this can underestimate the role of entropy in a complex free energy landscape. Quantifying transition rates between discrete resistance states during resistance fluctuations in nanoscale GeTe shows that entropic contributions can strongly shape the free energy barriers.
item.dc:title: A free energy landscape analysis of resistance fluctuations in a memristive device
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Walfort
Xuan Thang Vu
Jakob Ballmaier
Nils Holle
Niklas Vollmar
Martin Salinga

item.title: Scalable manufacture of nearly pure-phase metallic MoS<sub>2</sub> nanosheets
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 29 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02480-2

A microwave-assisted process is developed for the rapid and scalable manufacture of pure-phase metallic MoS2 nanosheets, enabling practical electrochemical devices for energy applications.
item.dc:title: Scalable manufacture of nearly pure-phase metallic MoS<sub>2</sub> nanosheets
item.dc:creator: Ziwei Jeffrey Yang
Zhuangnan Li
Leyi Loh
James Moloney
John Walmsley
Jiahang Li

Manish Chhowalla
